The Residences by DIFC mark the first residential chapter within the visionary DIFC Zabeel District, redefining contemporary urban living in the very heart of Dubai. Comprising two architecturally striking towers with sculptural silhouettes, the project introduces a refined residential destination seamlessly integrated into a dynamic mixed-use environment. Designed as an extension of DIFC’s prestigious legacy, the development blends business, culture, lifestyle, and leisure into a cohesive urban ecosystem, offering residents iconic views across Dubai’s most prominent landmarks. Residents of The Residences by DIFC enjoy a carefully curated collection of premium, hotel-inspired amenities designed to elevate everyday living. These include resort-style swimming pools, wellness and fitness facilities, dedicated yoga and relaxation spaces, and elegant residents’ lounges for socialising or quiet retreat. Thoughtfully designed communal areas provide spaces for work, leisure, and connection, while landscaped terraces and outdoor zones create a sense of calm within the urban setting. Complementing the private amenities, the project

Palace Residences Al Mamsha stands as a distinguished branded residential address, marking the first collaboration in Sharjah between Alef and Emaar Hospitality Group’s Palace brand. Positioned at the southern gateway of Al Mamsha, Sharjah’s first fully walkable community, the project reflects refined architectural elegance rooted in cultural identity and contemporary urban design. The twin residential towers, connected by a thoughtfully designed podium, create a landmark presence that blends openness, natural light, and greenery, echoing the region’s traditional courtyard-inspired living. The residences offer a selection of one-, two-, and three-bedroom apartments crafted with understated luxury and functional sophistication. Interiors are defined by serene palettes of white, grey, and natural wood, open-plan layouts, and soft textures that enhance comfort and connection. Bedrooms feature built-in wardrobes and warm finishes, while kitchens and bathrooms balance calming tones with premium materials, creating harmonious living spaces designed for everyday ease and refined living. Life at Palace Residences Al Mamsha centers around a community-driven lifestyle enriched by curated amenities and walkable surroundings. Residents enjoy green courtyards, sky terraces, family recreation areas, modern fitness facilities, adult and children’s pools, sky gardens, shaded walkways, and dedicated lounges. Retail and dining at the podium level add daily convenience, while the project’s prime location near Al Mamsha Souks, University City Road, and key urban destinations completes an elegant living experience shaped by connection, comfort, and timeless Palace hospitality. ##### Location description and benefits As the Cultural Capital of the Arab World, Sharjah proudly preserves its historical legacy. The Heart of Sharjah, a charming historic district, invites you to wander through its narrow alleys adorned with restored heritage buildings, such as the Al Hisn Fort and the Sharjah Heritage Museum. This commitment to cultural heritage

Tonino Lamborghini Residences by BNW Developments rises on Al Marjan Island as a bold architectural statement where Italian sophistication meets the raw beauty of the coast. Inspired by the Tonino Lamborghini ethos of elegance, innovation, and uncompromising style, the project translates the rhythm of waves and dunes into cascading terraces, sky gardens, and sculpted forms. Natural textures, refined metallic accents, and carefully proportioned balconies create a harmonious balance between privacy, openness, and uninterrupted sea views, forming a timeless coastal landmark. The residences are designed as an extension of the Tonino Lamborghini design language — confident, refined, and quietly expressive. Interiors feature flowing layouts that balance openness with intimate retreats, allowing living, dining, and private spaces to connect effortlessly. Warm neutral palettes, tactile materials, soft lighting, and meticulous detailing create an atmosphere of calm luxury, where comfort, craftsmanship, and clarity define everyday living in a setting shaped for both relaxation and refined hosting. Life at Tonino Lamborghini Residences unfolds through a curated collection of wellness and lifestyle amenities designed to support balance and wellbeing. Residents enjoy swimming pools, spa-inspired sauna and massage spaces, yoga areas, a modern gymnasium, landscaped Zen gardens, outdoor cinema, BBQ zones, children’s play and recreational areas, and elegant social lounges. Set within the serene island context of Al Marjan, the project offers a lifestyle where Italian design heritage, coastal tranquility, and contemporary luxury exist in perfect harmony. ##### Location description and benefits Al Marjan Island

These guidelines are designed to develop and control a specific architectural character for the project, in line with the general master plan theme and style. ##### Finishing and materials Materials Given the local climatic conditions, a well-constructed and insulated structure shall be provided. Materials used for construction should meet all requirements of the Dubai Green Building Regulations. Walls Horizontal and vertical elements shall be provided on exterior walls to provide architectural interest. This can be achieved through the creative use of windows, doors, parapets and even projections that cast shadows and provide visual relief to the buildings. Doors Composition of different heights and widths may be used, provided that the combination of individual elements appears homogeneous. The finishes shall be natural/painted timber/UPVC/powder-coated aluminium. In the latter case, the chosen colour must reflect the overall colour scheme of the building. Windows The arrangement of window openings should relate to individual proportions and grouping of windows, and the general proportions of the buildings. The size of the opening in the wall or a plane shall be determined by additional factors other than light, such as the materials and construction of the wall, requirements for visual privacy, ventilation and enclosure of the space, or the overall effect on the building’s exterior form and appearance. Balconies Balconies, big and small, functional or aesthetically placed, are encouraged. Figure 2.6 illustrates balconies and balustrades that shall be of glass railings, aluminium or stainless steel. Large balconies shall be either fully covered or semi-covered to protect the interiors from hot climatic conditions. ##### Location description and benefits Damac Hills 2
